title: quercus monnula
intro: Quercus monnula, also known as the Himalayan white oak, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Quercus monnula include reducing inflammation and pain, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus monnula can be found in the form of decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
